interviewStages: Behavioral: ownership, customer empathy, fast-moving AI startup
codingTopics: Streaming audio buffer processing with fixed windows (stream, streams, realtime, sliding-window, time-series)
Ring buffer for real-time audio frames with O(1) ops (cache, data structure, memory, realtime)
Transcript merging from overlapping inference windows (merge, string, streams)
Beam-search decoding over model output probabilities (heap, search, string)
Latency-bounded pipeline stage scheduling (pipeline, systems, monitoring)
systemDesignTopics: Design a real-time streaming speech-to-text API delivering sub-300ms partial transcripts over websockets
Design the model-serving layer that batches concurrent audio streams onto GPUs without hurting tail latency
Design an audio ingestion pipeline that normalizes, chunks, and routes many codecs for transcription at scale
Design a transcript post-processing service for diarization, punctuation, and formatting

coding: Implement a fixed-size ring buffer for incoming audio frames supporting O(1) writes/reads with no allocation on the hot path. Discuss producer/consumer concurrency.
You receive overlapping transcript hypotheses from consecutive audio windows; merge them into one consistent stream without duplicating words at the boundaries.
Implement beam search over per-timestep character probabilities to produce the top decoded transcript. Bound the beam width and analyze complexity.
Process a streaming audio signal in fixed windows and emit a voice-activity flag per window with bounded memory.
Given per-stage latency budgets (decode, inference, format), schedule frames so end-to-end p99 stays under a threshold and degrades gracefully under load.
behavioral: Tell me about optimizing a latency- or throughput-critical system. How did you measure and where did the wins come from?
Describe a hard performance bug in a real-time or ML system and how you tracked it down.
Tell me about taking a research idea and making it production-ready.
Describe owning an ambiguous problem in a small team and driving it end-to-end.
systemDesign: Design Deepgram's real-time streaming STT service: websocket ingestion, GPU inference, and sub-300ms partial results.
Design GPU batching that packs many concurrent audio streams while protecting tail latency.
Design an audio ingestion pipeline handling many codecs and sample rates at scale.
Design a diarization and formatting post-processor over raw model output.
values: Deepgram builds voice AI from the model up. What draws you to low-level, performance-heavy ML systems?
How do you balance model accuracy against latency and cost in a real-time product?
What excites you about voice as an interface versus text?
other: How does Deepgram compare to AssemblyAI or ElevenLabs?

Deepgram Interview Guide (2026): Speech AI Platform

On this page

- Process

- What they actually ask

- Levels and comp (2026)

- Prep priorities

- Frequently Asked Questions

Updated May 6, 2026 · techinterview.org

⏱ 1 min read

Deepgram
deepgram.com ↗

Deepgram is a leading speech-AI platform — ASR, voice agents, and a real-time streaming API used by enterprises and developers. Series C in 2024. The interview emphasizes speech-recognition systems engineering, real-time streaming infrastructure, and the engineering of multi-tenant ML inference.

Process

Recruiter screen → 60-minute coding (Python preferred for ML, Go for backend) → onsite virtual: 2 coding, 1 ML system design, 1 craft deep-dive, 1 behavioral. ML-research candidates get a research deep-dive. Cycle: 3–5 weeks.

What they actually ask

- Design a real-time ASR service with sub-300ms streaming latency

- Design a multi-tenant inference platform with billing per audio second

- Design a voice-agent platform combining ASR, LLM, TTS, and turn-taking

- Coding: medium DSA, often with audio or pipeline framing

- Behavioral: ownership, customer empathy, fast-moving AI startup

Levels and comp (2026)

- SE: $185K–$255K total (cash + meaningful equity)

- Senior SE: $260K–$355K total

- Staff: $360K–$510K total

- ML Research: $400K–$700K+ total at top of band

Prep priorities

- Be fluent in Python (research/serving) and Go (backend/control plane)

- Understand ASR architectures (CTC, RNN-T, attention) and streaming inference

- Brush up on audio codecs, voice-activity detection, and barge-in handling

Frequently Asked Questions

Is Deepgram remote-friendly?

Distributed since founding. Hub in San Francisco; engineers across US/EU.

How does Deepgram compare to AssemblyAI or ElevenLabs?

Deepgram is ASR-first with strong real-time streaming. AssemblyAI is ASR-first with broader speech intelligence (sentiment, entities). ElevenLabs is TTS-first. Comp competitive at top of band; Deepgram pays well for ML systems.

What is the engineering culture?

Technical, research-engineering blended. Strong customer focus on enterprise voice-AI deployments.
